title: Set 6 - Full TOEFL ITP Listening (50 Questions)
description: Complete TOEFL ITP Listening Comprehension test with Part A (short conversations), Part B (extended conversations), and Part C (talks and lectures)

questions.question: What does the woman mean?
questions.context: M: I heard the library extended its hours. W: Yes, now it's open until midnight on weekdays.

questions.question: What will the man probably do?
questions.context: W: The bookstore is closing in 10 minutes. M: Oh no, I need to buy my textbooks!

questions.question: What does the man suggest?
questions.context: W: I can't decide on an elective course. M: Why not take something related to your major?
choices: Choose a course in her field

questions.question: What does the woman imply?
questions.context: M: The concert tickets are $75. W: That's way beyond my budget.

questions.question: What are they discussing?
questions.context: M: Did you submit your scholarship application? W: Not yet, deadline is next Friday.

questions.question: What does the man mean?
questions.context: W: How was your chemistry exam? M: I should have studied more.

questions.question: What will the woman do?
questions.context: M: Are you going to the career fair? W: Definitely, I need to network.

questions.question: What does the woman suggest?
questions.context: M: I'm having trouble with statistics. W: Have you tried the tutoring center?

questions.question: What is the man's problem?
questions.context: M: My laptop crashed and I lost my essay. W: Didn't you back it up?

questions.question: What does the woman mean?
questions.context: M: Want to join the study group tonight? W: I would, but I have to work.

questions.question: What is the topic?
questions.context: M: The professor assigned three books. W: I know, that's a lot of reading for one week.

questions.question: What will they do?
questions.context: W: Should we form a study group for the midterm? M: Good idea, let's meet at the library.

questions.question: What does the man imply?
questions.context: W: I'm thinking of taking five courses next semester. M: That sounds overwhelming.

questions.question: What is the woman's concern?
questions.context: W: I'm worried about my presentation tomorrow. M: You'll do fine, you prepared well.

questions.question: What does the man suggest?
questions.context: W: I can't focus in the dorm. M: Try studying at the quiet floor of the library.

questions.question: What is the problem?
questions.context: M: All the parking spots are full. W: There's a lot two blocks away.

questions.question: What does the woman mean?
questions.context: M: Are you done with the group project? W: We're making progress, but still have work to do.

questions.question: What will the man do?
questions.context: W: Don't forget about the department meeting. M: Thanks for reminding me, I almost forgot.

questions.question: What are they talking about?
questions.context: M: I heard they're renovating the student center. W: Yes, it should be done by fall semester.

questions.question: What does the woman imply?
questions.context: M: Let's eat at the new cafeteria. W: I heard the prices are pretty high.

questions.question: What is the man's opinion?
questions.context: W: What did you think of the lecture? M: It was informative but too long.

questions.question: What does the woman suggest?
questions.context: M: I need to improve my writing. W: You should visit the writing center.

questions.question: What is the topic?
questions.context: M: Did you sign up for the field trip? W: Yes, I'm excited to visit the museum.

questions.question: What will they do?
questions.context: W: We need supplies for the experiment. M: I'll go to the lab supply room.

questions.question: What does the man mean?
questions.context: W: Are you joining the club? M: I'm interested, but I don't have time this semester.

questions.question: What is the problem?
questions.context: M: I can't log into my student account. W: You should contact IT support.

questions.question: What does the woman mean?
questions.context: M: Want to go to the game tonight? W: I have too much homework.

questions.question: What are they discussing?
questions.context: W: The professor changed the exam date. M: That's good, I need more time to prepare.

questions.question: What will the man do?
questions.context: W: Are you going to the review session? M: Absolutely, I don't want to miss it.

questions.question: What does the woman imply?
questions.context: M: How's your research paper going? W: I'm still gathering sources.

questions.question: What is the main topic?
questions.context: Extended conversation about campus housing options and dormitory rules.

questions.question: According to the conversation, what is required?
questions.context: Discussion continues about housing requirements.

questions.question: What does the speaker suggest?
questions.context: Advisor gives recommendations for housing.

questions.question: What can be inferred?
questions.context: Discussion about availability of housing.

questions.question: What will the student probably do?
questions.context: Student decides on housing action.

questions.question: What is the main topic?
questions.context: Extended conversation about internship opportunities in engineering.

questions.question: According to the speaker, what is important?
questions.context: Career advisor discusses internship requirements.

questions.question: When is the deadline?
questions.context: Discussion about application timeline.

questions.question: What does the advisor recommend?
questions.context: Advisor gives advice on applications.

questions.question: What will the student do next?
questions.context: Student plans next steps.

questions.question: What is the main topic of the lecture?
questions.context: Professor discusses renewable energy sources and sustainability.

questions.question: According to the professor, what is an advantage?
questions.context: Lecture continues on solar energy benefits.

questions.question: What example does the professor give?
questions.context: Professor provides case study.

questions.question: What can be inferred?
questions.context: Discussion of future energy trends.

questions.question: What does the professor emphasize?
questions.context: Professor stresses importance of action.

questions.question: What is the main topic?
questions.context: Lecture on cognitive psychology and memory.

questions.question: According to the lecture, what affects memory?
questions.context: Discussion of memory factors.

questions.question: What method is mentioned?
questions.context: Professor describes memory techniques.

questions.question: What does the professor suggest?
questions.context: Recommendations for students.

questions.question: What can be concluded?
questions.context: Lecture conclusion about memory.
